commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The hover interaction is restricted to a small div wrapper rather than the whole Card, leading to inconsistent hover effects.
issues.fix: Move the onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave handlers to the top-level Card component.

issues.description: The forecast list is rendered as a series of stacks without a semantic list structure or appropriate labels for screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap the forecast mapping in a semantic list element and provide aria-labels for the weather icon descriptions.

issues.description: Lack of CSS transitions for the shadow state change makes the interaction feel abrupt.
issues.fix: Add a transition property (e.g., 'transition: box-shadow 0.2s ease') to the Card style.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, functional, and correctly implements the required props using the provided design system. It could be improved by applying the hover state to the entire container rather than a partial wrapper and adding semantic structure to the forecast list for better accessibility.

issues.description: The hover-state logic is applied only to a div wrapper around the humidity/wind section, causing the shadow effect to feel disjointed from the card container.
issues.fix: Move the onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave handlers to the top-level Card component.

issues.description: The current forecast icons are using the primary condition icon (props.condition) rather than the dynamic icons requested in the forecast array.
issues.fix: Use the f.icon property inside the forecast map loop for the Icon component.

issues.description: Lack of ARIA labels on informative elements like weather conditions and humidity metrics.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the Humidity and Wind indicator containers to describe their purpose for screen readers.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and adheres to the design system constraints. The primary areas for improvement involve syncing the forecast icon display with the provided data and improving the interaction UX by applying hover states to the whole card.

issues.description: The hover-state logic is applied only to a div wrapper around the humidity/wind section, causing the shadow effect to feel disjointed from the card container.
issues.fix: Move the onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave handlers to the top-level Card component.

issues.description: The current forecast icons are using the primary condition icon (props.condition) rather than the dynamic icons requested in the forecast array.
issues.fix: Use the f.icon property inside the forecast map loop for the Icon component.

issues.description: Lack of ARIA labels on informative elements like weather conditions and humidity metrics.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the Humidity and Wind indicator containers to describe their purpose for screen readers.

evaluation.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and adheres to the design system constraints. The primary areas for improvement involve syncing the forecast icon display with the provided data and improving the interaction UX by applying hover states to the whole card.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: The 'Add Task' form is missing the priority selector dropdown requested in the requirements.
issues.fix: Add a Select component inside the 'Add Task' form to allow users to choose priority (low, medium, high) when creating a task.

issues.description: Tasks cannot be edited via double-click as specified in the original requirements; the current implementation only allows creation and moving.
issues.fix: Implement an editable state (or a modal/popover) to handle renaming task titles on double-click or via an edit button.

issues.description: The move action is triggered via an onChange event on a select menu, which may be confusing for screen reader users without explicit aria-labels.
issues.fix: Add explicit aria-labels to the move Select components and ensure task headings use semantic tags.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and leverages the provided design system effectively. While it meets the core layout and movement requirements, it misses a few functional specifications like the priority selector in the create form and the double-click editing feature. Overall, it provides a solid foundation with professional UI aesthetics.

issues.description: The card layout is a bit cramped, and the 'Move' select dropdown consumes too much vertical space within each card.
issues.fix: Use a smaller select component or a compact menu/popover for the 'move' action to reduce visual clutter on task cards.

issues.description: Lack of loading states or optimistic UI feedback when moving tasks.
issues.fix: Add a subtle transition or skeleton state if onTaskUpdate involves async processing.

issues.description: Double-click for editing may be undiscoverable for screen reader users or those without mice.
issues.fix: Include an explicit edit button icon on the task card as a primary way to trigger editing.

evaluations.critique: The implementation is robust and functional, effectively utilizing the provided primitives to create a clean Kanban interface. The state management logic is well-organized, though the UI could benefit from more refined interactive patterns, such as dedicated edit buttons instead of relying solely on double-click events.

issues.fix: Add a subtle transition or skeleton state if onTaskUpdate involves async processing.

issues.fix: Include an explicit edit button icon on the task card as a primary way to trigger editing.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.fix: Create a mapping function or object to assign a specific 'var(--ggui-color-...)' or background-color based on the 'category' string for each element card.

issues.description: The legend is implemented as a list of tags but lacks the color indicator required by the prompt.
issues.fix: Add a small colored indicator/swatch to each tag in the legend to show which category it represents.

issues.description: The custom button wrapper for elements lacks an aria-label or accessible name.
issues.fix: Add aria-label={`Select ${el.name}`} to the button element.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional and well-structured, successfully implementing the complex grid layout required for the periodic table. However, it fails to implement the specific color-coding requirement for element categories, which is critical for visual clarity in a periodic table, and the legend lacks the visual connection to those missing colors.

issues.description: The periodic table grid visualizes the missing cells as empty space, which can look disjointed when filtering.
issues.fix: Consider adding a subtle placeholder for empty grid cells or refining the grid layout logic to handle filtered states more gracefully.

issues.description: Buttons representing elements lack explicit aria-labels for screen readers.
issues.fix: Add aria-label={`Select ${el.name}`} to the button wrapping the element card.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and maps effectively to the provided data contract. The use of CSS Grid and React hooks is idiomatic and clean. To improve, it would benefit from more distinct visual contrast between the active selection and the grid, and improved labeling for accessibility.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

example.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
example.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

addToCart.label: Add to Cart
addToCart.description: Called when user clicks Add to Cart. Payload: { productId, quantity }

product.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
product.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

reviews.author: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Great sound quality. A bit tight for my head but breaks in after a week.

reviews.author: James P.
reviews.text: Battery life is exactly as advertised. Very impressed.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Visual feedback for errors in Step 1 is missing, meaning the user won't know why 'Next' is disabled.
issues.fix: Pass the nameError and emailError strings into the FormField 'error' prop (if available) or render a conditional helper Text component below the inputs.

issues.description: The avatar buttons lack explicit labels, relying only on emojis.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the avatar selection buttons (e.g., `aria-label={`Select avatar ${emoji}`}`).

issues.description: No transition or animation between steps; the content snaps instantly.
issues.fix: Wrap step contents in a simple transition or use a motion component if available in the library to provide a smoother UX.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional, clean, and follows the provided contract perfectly. It handles state management effectively and provides a logical flow for the user. Adding inline validation messaging and ARIA labels would elevate it to a professional, production-ready standard.
